mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or earnings growth decouples from industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% during a period of accelerating inflation, real rates turn restrictive, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or expand despite real rates exceeding 3%, or forward returns show no statistically significant decline during high-rate/high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show negative correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing permits below 1.5 million units signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing permits drop below 1.5 million, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and rising consumer credit balances indicate expanding household balance sheets, which fuels consumption-driven earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict earnings revisions directionally, or forward returns show no significant premium during periods of rising credit and employment.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces labor force growth, unit labor costs compress, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > PAYEMS month-over-month change
failure_condition: Profit margins contract despite output growing faster than employment, or the signal shows negative predictive power for equity returns over any 12-month rolling window.
